title: Runner, replayable logs, pytest plugin, observability

tasks.criterion: Async runner returns an EvalLog for a no-op task: test_run_noop imports asyncio and trine_eval.runner.engine.run, creates a minimal Task with one Sample, calls asyncio.run(run(task, model)) with a stub model, and asserts the return value is an EvalLog with len(scores) == 1.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/runner/test_engine.py -k test_run_noop -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/s02c1.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/s02c1.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: Hard-cap enforcement — all four caps fire correctly: tests/runner/test_caps.py contains at least four independent tests (one per cap). Three tests (token_limit, time_limit, cost_limit) assert EvalLog.metadata['cap_hit'] names the triggered cap and the log is partial. One test (max_concurrency) verifies bounded concurrency via an in-flight counter without asserting cap_hit, because max_concurrency only throttles parallelism and does not cause early abort.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/runner/test_caps.py -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/s02c2.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/s02c2.txt && grep -E "4 passed|[5-9] passed|[1-9][0-9]+ passed" /tmp/s02c2.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: Log round-trip — JSON and msgpack: tests/runner/test_logformat.py contains test_json_round_trip and test_msgpack_round_trip; each saves a non-trivial EvalLog (>=2 samples, non-empty aggregate) and asserts load(save(log)) == log via Pydantic equality.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/runner/test_logformat.py -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/s02c3.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/s02c3.txt && grep -E "2 passed|[3-9] passed|[1-9][0-9]+ passed" /tmp/s02c3.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: pytest plugin exits 100 on failing trine-eval test: uv run pytest tests/runner/test_plugin_exit.py exits with code 100 (not 1) when a trine-eval score falls below the configured threshold.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/runner/test_plugin_exit.py --tb=short 2>&1; CODE=$?; [ "$CODE" = "100" ] && echo PLUGIN_OK || (echo "Got exit $CODE expected 100" && exit 1)'

tasks.criterion: trine-eval score --log <path> --scorer <name> rescores without re-running model: test saves a fixture EvalLog, calls the CLI subcommand via subprocess, asserts exit 0 and a score value in stdout, and confirms no real Anthropic API call was made.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/runner/test_rescore_cli.py -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/s02c5.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/s02c5.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: trine-eval report <log> prints token-efficiency metrics: test calls the CLI subcommand via subprocess and asserts exit 0 and stdout contains accuracy_per_dollar and success_per_1k_tokens (case-insensitive, hyphens or underscores accepted).
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/runner/test_report_cli.py -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/s02c6.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/s02c6.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: OTel + Langfuse wiring composes without errors: tests/observability/test_otel.py imports trine_eval.observability.otel, calls init_tracer(exporter='noop') without raising, starts/ends a span, and calls get_langfuse_client() with stub credentials without making network calls.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/observability/test_otel.py -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/s02c7.txt && grep -q "PASSED" /tmp/s02c7.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: ops/langfuse-compose.yaml is valid YAML with a 'services' key: the file must exist and parse as a dict containing a 'services' key. Missing file → FileNotFoundError → non-zero exit → FAIL. Present but missing services key → sys.exit(1) → FAIL.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'python -c "import sys, yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open(\"ops/langfuse-compose.yaml\")); sys.exit(0 if isinstance(d, dict) and \"services\" in d else 1)" && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: phase-02-N key convention documented in rules/harness-conventions.md: the file contains the literal strings 'phase-02-N', 'current_sprint', and 'current_phase' in the new subsection, documenting how current_phase disambiguates what current_sprint refers to.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'grep -q "phase-02-N" rules/harness-conventions.md && grep -q "current_sprint" rules/harness-conventions.md && grep -q "current_phase" rules/harness-conventions.md && echo PASS || echo FAIL'

tasks.criterion: All new tests collected and green — full runner + observability suite: uv run pytest tests/runner/ tests/observability/ exits 0 with no failures.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'uv run pytest tests/runner/ tests/observability/ -v --tb=short 2>&1 | tee /tmp/s02c10.txt && grep -E "passed" /tmp/s02c10.txt && echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: Async runner hard-cap design is correct: src/trine_eval/runner/engine.py uses asyncio.Semaphore for max_concurrency, accumulates tokens and aborts on token_limit, enforces time_limit via wall-clock, computes USD cost from Opus 4.7 pricing for cost_limit, and embeds 'cap_hit' in EvalLog.metadata. Stubs or single-cap enforcement is FAIL.

pyproject.toml dependency additions are complete: runtime deps include msgpack>=1.0, opentelemetry-api>=1.20, opentelemetry-sdk>=1.20, langfuse>=2.0; [project.entry-points.pytest11] declares trine-eval = 'trine_eval.pytest_plugin'; no forbidden packages (langgraph, ragas, pgvector, fastapi) appear anywhere.

Historical Phase 1 contracts and evals are unmodified: no file matching .harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-9]*.md, .harness/contracts/sprint-1*.md, .harness/evals/sprint-0[1-9]*.md, or .harness/evals/sprint-1*.md is modified on this branch.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'BASE=$(git merge-base HEAD main 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse HEAD~$(git rev-list --count HEAD)^); diff_out=$(git diff $BASE..HEAD -- ".harness/contracts/sprint-0[1-9]*.md" ".harness/contracts/sprint-1*.md" ".harness/evals/sprint-0[1-9]*.md" ".harness/evals/sprint-1*.md" 2>/dev/null); [ -z "$diff_out" ] && echo PASS || (echo FAIL && echo "$diff_out" && exit 1)'

tasks.criterion: No forbidden packages imported or declared: langgraph, ragas, pgvector, fastapi do not appear in pyproject.toml, src/trine_eval/**/*.py, or tests/**/*.py.
tasks.verification_command: bash -c 'git grep -rn --count -E "langgraph|ragas|pgvector|fastapi" pyproject.toml src/ tests/ 2>/dev/null | grep -v "^Binary" | grep -v ":0$" && echo FAIL && exit 1 || echo PASS'

tasks.criterion: No real Anthropic API calls in tests: the Evaluator must read test source files (tests/runner/test_engine.py, tests/runner/test_caps.py, tests/runner/test_rescore_cli.py, and any other file importing anthropic) and verify every anthropic.Anthropic(...) instantiation is enclosed in a mocker.patch(...) block or unittest.mock.patch(...) context. A bare anthropic.Anthropic() call outside a patch context is FAIL.
